title: The Console & Output 🖨️
description: Master console.log and learn how to display messages.

slides.title: The Developer Console 🛠️
slides.content:
# Your Secret Weapon

The **developer console** is a hidden tool built into every web browser. It's where JavaScript developers:

* 🔍 **Test code** snippets quickly
* 🐛 **Debug** problems
* 📊 **Inspect** data and variables
* 📝 **View** log messages

Think of the console as your **scratch pad** — a place to experiment without breaking anything. Every pro developer uses it daily!

slides.title: Console Methods: Beyond .log() 🎨
slides.content:
## console Has Many Superpowers!

`console.log()` is just the beginning. The `console` object has several useful methods:

* **`console.log()`** → Standard output. Your everyday printer. 📄
* **`console.warn()`** → Prints a yellow ⚠️ warning message.
* **`console.error()`** → Prints a red ❌ error message.
* **`console.info()`** → Prints an informational ℹ️ message.
* **`console.clear()`** → Wipes the console clean. 🧹

Each method **colors the output differently** in the browser console, making it easy to spot important messages at a glance.

slides.title: See Them in Action 🎯
slides.content: Try running these different console methods and notice how each one looks different:

quizData.question: Which console method displays a warning message?
quizData.explanation: console.warn() displays a yellow warning message in the console. console.alert() doesn't exist!

slides.title: Printing Multiple Values 📦
Multiple Values Example 💡
slides.content: See how commas separate multiple values and JavaScript adds spaces automatically:

quizData.question: What is the output of: console.log("X:", 10)?
quizData.explanation: When you pass multiple values separated by commas, console.log() automatically inserts a space between them.

slides.title: Escape Characters 🏃
slides.content:
## Special Characters in Strings

Sometimes you need to include special characters in your text. JavaScript uses the **backslash** (`\`) as an escape character:

* **`\n`** → New line (moves to next line) ↵
* **`\t`** → Tab (adds a big space) ⇥
* **`\\`** → Prints a literal backslash `\`
* **`\"`** → Prints a double quote inside double-quoted strings
* **`\'`** → Prints a single quote inside single-quoted strings

Think of `\` as a **secret code prefix** — it tells JavaScript "the next character is special, don't treat it normally!"

slides.title: Escape Characters in Action 💻
slides.content: See how escape characters change the output:

quizData.question: What does the escape character \n do?
quizData.explanation: \n is the newline escape character — it moves the output to the next line.

slides.title: Pro Tip: console.table() 🚀
slides.content:
## Display Data Beautifully

JavaScript has a secret weapon for displaying organized data: `console.table()`!

It takes an array or object and displays it as a **beautiful table** in the console. We'll learn about arrays and objects later, but here's a sneak peek of how clean it looks:

```javascript
console.table(["Apple", "Banana", "Cherry"]);
```

Keep this one in your back pocket — you'll love it when working with data! 📊
